# Vais Reference App Contract

Last reviewed: 2026-05-25

The reference app is the end-to-end validation target for AI-written Vais code.
It should be small enough for a new AI agent to understand from official docs,
but broad enough to exercise the language, compiler, DB, server, web, and
playground boundaries that public docs mention.

## Required Coverage

- `canonical-syntax`: Use full-word declarations where available and compact control forms where they remain the current language surface.
- `strict-type-boundaries`: Numeric adaptation is allowed in numeric contexts, but bool, str, structural types, and typed pointers do not silently become i64.
- `generic-enum-layout`: Option&lt;T&gt; and Result&lt;T, E&gt; use the canonical erased runtime enum layout with an i32 tag and payload storage.
- `result-option-flow`: Result&lt;T, E&gt;, Option&lt;T&gt;, match, the try operator, and unwrap-style lowering are explicit language surfaces.
- `references-pointers-slices`: References, raw pointers, and slices are distinct type surfaces with explicit conversions and explicit dereference expectations.
- `ecosystem-evidence-boundary`: DB, server, and web behavior is public only where named gates cover it. Do not describe the ecosystem as product-complete.
- `verification-first-development`: AI-written Vais code must be checked against the compiler and named gates instead of relying on model memory.

## Suggested App Shape

- A task or notes service with list, detail, create, update, and delete flows.
- A schema fixture that can be reused by compiler, DB, server, and web tests.
- Result-returning validation functions and Option-returning lookup functions.
- Match-heavy control flow for empty, found, validation-error, and storage-error
  cases.
- At least one explicit pointer/raw-address boundary test if low-level APIs are
  involved.
- A playground example extracted from the same source pattern.

## Current Reference App

Name: AI docs notes service

Path: `tests/ai_reference_app/notes_service.vais`

Gate: `scripts/check-ai-reference-app.sh`

Small canonical Vais program that a new AI agent can reconstruct from docs/ai and docs/LANGUAGE_SPEC.md without relying on model memory.

## Acceptance Gates

```bash
cargo fmt --all -- --check
cargo test -p vais-types --test inference_unification_tests -- --nocapture
cargo test -p vaisc --test e2e -- --nocapture
node scripts/check-public-claims.mjs
node scripts/check-ai-docs-sync.mjs
bash scripts/check-ai-reference-app.sh
```

The reference app is complete only when a clean checkout can build and test it
without relying on unstated AI memory.
